description: A Taoist architectural complex from the Ming Dynasty, dedicated to Lü Dongbin, featuring exquisite architecture and rich cultural heritage.
China's first museum dedicated to bronze artifacts, showcasing exquisite pieces of bronze culture.
The riverside landscape belt of Taiyuan's mother river, with waterfront green spaces, ideal for leisurely walks and experiencing the modern charm of Taiyuan.
An ancient Buddhist temple with a serene environment, a great destination for hiking and worship.

attraction_name: Taiyuan Changfeng Cultural and Business District
description: Taiyuan's modern business core area, offering beautiful night views, perfect for leisurely strolls.
A Qing Dynasty bank building, showcasing the development of traditional finance, contrasting with the Rishengchang Exchange House.
Taiyuan's historical and cultural district, combining traditional architecture with modern commerce, offering an experience of Jin merchants' culture.
A museum dedicated to the history of Jin merchants' culture, based on the former site of the Shanxi Military Governor's Office.
An ancient Buddhist temple from the Ming Dynasty, well-preserved with renowned exquisite sculptures and murals.
